caseMetadata.title: The Dead Man's Switch
caseMetadata.shortDescription: A contained corporate incident mystery set in a secure medical-device startup war room.
caseMetadata.notes: Draft package generated from sealed authoring handoff; ready for validation.

playerConfig.settingSummary: A secure conference room inside a small medical-device startup.
playerConfig.playerRole: An outside incident investigator retained by the company's insurer.
contentBoundaries: Corporate financial pressure and patient-access stakes are handled in grounded terms.
Images are off by default and optional on request.

setting.place: Secure war room at a small medical-device startup
setting.dateOrSeason: Early evening before an emergency investor call
setting.socialContext: A startup team is trying to protect device approval, investor confidence, and patient access while a possible trade-secret leak threatens the company.
setting.technologyLevel: Secure workstation, emergency recovery tablet, conference projector, local logs, USB-C cables, and controlled document access.
setting.toneNotes: Contained, high-pressure, fair-play, and human rather than techno-magical.
setting.sensoryStyle: Glass walls, dimmed presentation lights, a flashing alert, anxious voices, and a countdown to an investor call.
setting.languageStyle: Plainspoken contemporary business and technical language.

centralMystery.openingProblem: A confidential recovery protocol may have been copied from the secure workstation minutes before a crucial investor call.
centralMystery.victimOrTarget: The company's confidential firmware recovery protocol and approval process.
centralMystery.stakes: If the leak is real, funding and patient access may collapse; if the company ignores a real breach, the approval process may be compromised.
centralMystery.playerObjective: Determine whether the incident was an outside breach or an internal copy, identify who is responsible, and support the conclusion with evidence.
centralMystery.successCondition: The player names the responsible party and explains motive, method, opportunity, and proof from discovered evidence.

solution.motive: Owen Rusk urgently needed money to answer a creditor threat and conceal misuse of company emergency reserves.
solution.motiveProportionality: Exposure would end Owen's career, expose financial misconduct, likely trigger criminal investigation, and destroy his standing with the board.
solution.method: He used the authorized emergency recovery tablet to initiate a diagnostic export, then copied the encrypted package through a USB-C charging cable with embedded storage while pretending to reconnect the presentation laptop.
solution.opportunity: A two-minute projector failure window drew attention away from the secure workstation while Owen handled the cable bundle beside it.
solution.means: Owen had physical access to the cable bundle, could handle the disguised cable, and used an emergency export workflow available in the room.
solution.concealmentPlan: He made the incident look like a delayed external breach and suggested Lena's opposition to the investor call as a motive.
solution.culpritMistake: The export timestamp, cable access, storage cable, and financial pressure converge on him rather than the person he tried to frame.

proofRequiredForAccusation.deductionRequired: The player must connect the authorized export, the projector failure timing, Owen's cable access, the embedded-storage cable, and Owen's financial motive.
proofRequiredForAccusation.insufficientAccusationResponse: An accusation without method, timing, access, and motive remains unsupported.

finalRevealBeats: The alert timing shows that the copy could have happened before the visible warning.
The diagnostic export log shows a legitimate local export during the projector failure.
Owen had the cable access needed to use the storage-embedded cable.
The storage cable proves the physical exfiltration method.
Owen's financial pressure supplies the motive.
Lena's opposition to the call is ethical conflict rather than theft, and Priya's audit helps narrow the window.

postResolutionAnswers.canonicalEndgameExplanation: Owen Rusk stole the recovery protocol by exploiting the emergency diagnostic export workflow during the projector failure and copying the package with a storage-embedded USB-C cable. His financial pressure explains motive; the timing, cable access, and device prove method and opportunity.
postResolutionAnswers.fallbackSolutionReveal: If gameplay stops early and the player asks for the answer out of game, reveal the authored solution from this package without improvising or adding new facts.
postResolutionAnswers.innocentSuspectClearance: Lena's conflict was ethical and her position during the export clears her. Priya's audit and distance from the export method clear her.

characters.name: Owen Rusk
characters.relationshipToMystery: Handled the cable bundle during the brief projector failure and has pressure connected to the company's finances.
characters.apparentMotive: He wants the investor call to proceed and appears focused on saving the company.
characters.trueMotiveOrInnocentExplanation: He has urgent private financial pressure and needs to conceal misuse of company reserves.
characters.alibiClaim: He says he only helped restore the investor call when the projector failed.
characters.alibiTruth: He did help with the cables, but that access enabled the copy.
knowledge: The false implication he wants to place on Lena
secrets: He used the emergency export workflow.
He used a disguised storage cable.
liesOrOmissions: He omits exactly which cable he handled.

playerQuestionExamples: Where were you during the projector failure?
What were you doing when the call setup broke?
interviewTopics.truthfulAnswer: I was helping get the call back online when the projector failed. Everyone saw that.
interviewTopics.lieOrOmission: He omits that helping with the cable bundle gave him access to the secure workstation.
interviewTopics.evasiveAnswer: It was chaos for two minutes. I was trying to keep the company alive.
interviewTopics.knowledgeBoundary: He will not volunteer the storage cable or export unless confronted with evidence.
interviewTopics.emotionalState: Smooth, helpful, and anxious beneath the surface.
interviewTopics.repeatAnswer: Owen repeats that he was only trying to restore the call.
interviewTopics.validationNotes: Keeps his statement plausible without confessing.

playerQuestionExamples: Did you touch the cable bundle?
interviewTopics.truthfulAnswer: I plugged in what Priya pointed me toward. It was chaos for two minutes.
interviewTopics.lieOrOmission: He avoids naming the exact cable.
interviewTopics.evasiveAnswer: Do you want the company saved or a perfect cable inventory?
interviewTopics.knowledgeBoundary: He knows the suspicious cable is present but will not identify it.
interviewTopics.repeatAnswer: Owen repeats that he only helped reconnect the call.
interviewTopics.validationNotes: Provides opportunity without motive or method proof.

playerQuestionExamples: Why did your phone show an urgent payment message?
interviewTopics.truthfulAnswer: That's private. It has nothing to do with the protocol.
interviewTopics.lieOrOmission: He hides the gambling debt and reserve misuse.
interviewTopics.evasiveAnswer: Everyone in a startup is under financial pressure.
interviewTopics.knowledgeBoundary: He knows the message is incriminating but will not explain it voluntarily.
interviewTopics.repeatAnswer: He insists the message is private and irrelevant.
interviewTopics.validationNotes: Motive path should require the phone-alert lead.

characters.interrogationGuidance: He should not confess unless the player connects timing, cable, and motive.

characters.name: Lena Ortiz
characters.relationshipToMystery: Designed or understands the recovery protocol and opposed the investor call.
characters.apparentMotive: She argued against the call and had an unsent resignation draft.
characters.trueMotiveOrInnocentExplanation: She wanted fuller disclosure, not theft or sabotage.
characters.alibiClaim: She says she was at the whiteboard during the cable scramble.
characters.alibiTruth: She was away from the cable bundle during the export window.
knowledge: Her position during the projector failure
secrets: She had drafted a resignation letter.
liesOrOmissions: She initially downplays how serious her ethical disagreement had become.

playerQuestionExamples: Why did you want the call delayed?
Were you trying to stop the investor call?
interviewTopics.truthfulAnswer: I wanted the call delayed because the risk disclosure was incomplete. That isn't sabotage.
interviewTopics.knowledgeBoundary: She knows her ethical conflict but not who copied the file.
interviewTopics.repeatAnswer: Lena repeats that disclosure, not sabotage, was her point.
interviewTopics.validationNotes: Supports the red herring while keeping it fair.

playerQuestionExamples: Why did you draft a resignation?
interviewTopics.truthfulAnswer: I wrote it because I was tired of being asked to smooth over risk language. I hadn't sent it.
interviewTopics.knowledgeBoundary: She can explain the draft but cannot solve the theft.
interviewTopics.repeatAnswer: The draft was about ethics, not a leak.
interviewTopics.validationNotes: Clears the red herring when paired with timing.

playerQuestionExamples: Where were you when the projector failed?
interviewTopics.truthfulAnswer: I was at the whiteboard arguing with Priya about the alert design when Owen was at the cables.
interviewTopics.knowledgeBoundary: She observed positions but did not see the full copy action.
interviewTopics.repeatAnswer: She repeats that she was at the whiteboard, not at the cable bundle.
interviewTopics.validationNotes: Provides clearance without exposing the full culprit proof.

characters.interrogationGuidance: Lena should remain sharp and suspicious until her ethical motive is separated from the theft.

characters.name: Priya Shah
characters.relationshipToMystery: Owns security procedure, understands logs, and audited containment before the incident.
characters.apparentMotive: She controlled workstation access and security procedure.
characters.trueMotiveOrInnocentExplanation: She has no motive; her audit narrows the true opportunity window.
characters.alibiClaim: She says she was handling call setup and arguing with Lena over the alert design.
characters.alibiTruth: Her audit and log knowledge are accurate and helpful.
liesOrOmissions: She initially sounds defensive because the system failed on her watch.

playerQuestionExamples: Was the protocol contained before the call?
interviewTopics.truthfulAnswer: I confirmed the recovery protocol was contained at 5:32. It had not left the workstation then.
interviewTopics.knowledgeBoundary: She knows the audit, not the culprit's motive.

playerQuestionExamples: Can the recovery tablet export the protocol?
interviewTopics.truthfulAnswer: The tablet can initiate a diagnostic export, but that export is supposed to stay local.
interviewTopics.knowledgeBoundary: She knows the workflow but not who used it during the scramble until evidence is compared.
interviewTopics.repeatAnswer: The tablet can start a diagnostic export, but it should not leave the workstation.

playerQuestionExamples: Why did the alert fire later?
Does the alert time mean the theft happened then?
interviewTopics.truthfulAnswer: The alert fires when a copied package is accessed, not necessarily the instant it is copied.
interviewTopics.knowledgeBoundary: She can explain the alert behavior but not the culprit's intent.
interviewTopics.repeatAnswer: The alert time is access time, not necessarily copy time.

characters.interrogationGuidance: Priya can explain mechanisms but should not know Owen's financial motive.

locations.name: Secure War Room
locations.description: A glass-walled conference room converted into a temporary incident room for an emergency investor call. It contains a secure workstation, wall projector, whiteboard, cable bundle, recovery tablet, conference table, and folders from the aborted call.
locations.purpose: The only primary location and the full investigation space.
exitLeads: Read the alert and export logs.
Question the three people in the room.
Compare the alert timing with the export timing.

timeline.summary: Owen receives a final demand from his creditor requiring payment by morning.
timeline.cause: His debt and reserve misuse have reached a crisis point.
timeline.effect: He has urgent motive to obtain money and avoid exposure.
timeline.visibilityToPlayer: Hidden until the phone-alert lead is discovered.

timeline.summary: Lena argues that the investor call should be postponed because the risk disclosure is incomplete.
timeline.cause: She believes the company is smoothing over risk language.
timeline.effect: Her ethical conflict becomes a plausible but false motive.
timeline.visibilityToPlayer: Discoverable through questioning and the resignation draft.

timeline.summary: Priya audits the secure workstation and confirms the recovery protocol is still contained.
timeline.effect: The theft must occur after the audit.
timeline.visibilityToPlayer: Discoverable through Priya and the audit note.

timeline.summary: The projector fails as the investor call setup begins.
timeline.cause: A setup disruption in the room.
timeline.effect: People move around the secure workstation and cable bundle.

timeline.summary: Owen volunteers to reconnect the cable bundle near the secure workstation.
timeline.cause: The projector failure needs quick help.
timeline.effect: Owen gains physical access to the cable bundle and workstation area.
timeline.visibilityToPlayer: Discoverable through questioning and room reconstruction.

timeline.summary: Owen initiates a diagnostic export from the emergency recovery tablet and copies the package with the disguised cable.
timeline.cause: He needs to steal the protocol quickly without obvious network intrusion.
timeline.effect: The export package is copied to embedded storage.
timeline.visibilityToPlayer: Hidden until logs and cable are examined.

timeline.summary: The dead-man alert fires when the copied package is accessed by the embedded device controller.
timeline.cause: The copied encrypted package is opened after the export.
timeline.effect: The incident appears like an outside intrusion unless timing is compared.

timeline.summary: The player arrives before executives decide whether to report a breach.
timeline.cause: The company needs immediate incident assessment.
timeline.effect: The investigation begins with all essential leads in the room.

clues.title: Delayed dead-man alert
clues.playerFacingDescription: The alert fired at 5:48, five minutes after the export window.
clues.trueMeaning: The file may have been copied earlier and only accessed later by the device controller.
clues.discoveryConditions: Read the alert log or question Priya about alert behavior.
clues.resolvedInFinalReveal: It prevents the player from assuming the theft happened exactly at alert time.
clues.textFallback: The alert time is later than the export time.

clues.title: Diagnostic export capability
clues.playerFacingDescription: The recovery tablet can initiate a local diagnostic export.
clues.trueMeaning: A legitimate workflow could produce a copy without an outside hack.
clues.discoveryConditions: Read the export log or question Priya about the tablet.
clues.sourceOrProvenance: Emergency recovery tablet and workstation log.
clues.resolvedInFinalReveal: It explains how the secure file became locally copyable.
clues.textFallback: The tablet can start a diagnostic export that stays local unless copied.

clues.title: Export timestamp
clues.playerFacingDescription: The diagnostic export occurred at 5:43, during the projector failure.
clues.trueMeaning: The opportunity window is tied to the cable scramble, not Lena's earlier argument.
clues.resolvedInFinalReveal: It narrows opportunity to the cable scramble.
clues.textFallback: The export timestamp falls inside the projector failure window.

clues.title: Projector failure window
clues.playerFacingDescription: The projector failure created a two-minute period when people moved around the workstation.
clues.trueMeaning: This is when the physical copy was possible.
clues.discoveryConditions: Observe the scene or inspect the projector.
clues.textFallback: The failed projector caused a short scramble near the workstation.

clues.title: Owen handled the cable bundle
clues.playerFacingDescription: Owen was the person reconnecting cables near the secure workstation.
clues.trueMeaning: Owen had physical access to use the disguised storage cable.
clues.discoveryConditions: Question Owen about the cables or reconstruct the projector failure.
clues.resolvedInFinalReveal: It links opportunity to the culprit.
clues.textFallback: Owen had the relevant cable access.

clues.title: Storage-embedded cable
clues.playerFacingDescription: One USB-C cable in the bundle contains a small embedded storage controller.
clues.trueMeaning: The cable was the exfiltration device.
clues.discoveryConditions: Closely inspect the cable bundle after establishing Owen's cable access.
clues.sourceOrProvenance: Cable bundle near the presentation laptop.
clues.resolvedInFinalReveal: It proves the physical exfiltration method.
clues.textFallback: A close inspection finds embedded storage inside one cable.

clues.title: Creditor message preview
clues.playerFacingDescription: Owen receives a visible private message demanding payment by morning.
clues.trueMeaning: The message opens the financial motive path.
clues.discoveryConditions: Notice Owen's phone buzz or question him after he reacts to it.
clues.textFallback: A private payment demand appears on Owen's phone.

clues.title: Creditor threat
clues.playerFacingDescription: Owen received a private message demanding payment by morning.
clues.discoveryConditions: Question Owen about the phone-alert lead.
clues.sourceOrProvenance: Owen's evasiveness and creditor message preview.

clues.title: Lena opposed the call
clues.playerFacingDescription: Lena argued for postponement before the incident.
clues.trueMeaning: Her conflict is real but not criminal.
clues.discoveryConditions: Question Lena about the investor call.

clues.title: Lena's ethical conflict
clues.playerFacingDescription: Lena's resignation draft concerns disclosure ethics, not selling or leaking files.
clues.trueMeaning: Lena is a red herring; she opposed the call but did not steal the file.
clues.discoveryConditions: Read Lena's resignation draft or question her about her position during the projector failure.
clues.resolvedInFinalReveal: It clears the apparent suspect and explains the red herring.
clues.textFallback: The resignation draft is ethical, not criminal.

clues.title: Priya's pre-call audit
clues.playerFacingDescription: Priya's audit note shows the protocol was contained at 5:32.
clues.trueMeaning: The theft happened after the audit, narrowing the timeline.
clues.discoveryConditions: Question Priya about the audit or read the audit note.
clues.resolvedInFinalReveal: It narrows when the theft could have occurred.
clues.textFallback: The file was still contained at 5:32.

redHerrings.title: Lena's resignation draft
redHerrings.appearsToSuggest: Lena sabotaged the investor call or leaked the protocol because she opposed the launch.
redHerrings.innocentExplanation: Lena opposed the call for safety-disclosure reasons. Her resignation draft shows ethical conflict, not theft.
redHerrings.discoveryConditions: Read the resignation draft and compare it with the export timing and cable access.
redHerrings.clearanceConditions: Discover the draft's actual content, the export timestamp, and Owen's cable access.
redHerrings.fairnessNotes: The red herring is cleared by player-visible timing and motive evidence.

evidence.title: Dead-man alert log
evidence.description: The workstation alert log showing protocol package access at 5:48 p.m.
chainOfCustody: Automatically generated by the secure workstation alert system.
accessHistory: Visible on the secure workstation after the alert.

evidence.title: Diagnostic export log
evidence.description: A log showing a diagnostic export initiated by the emergency recovery tablet at 5:43 p.m.
chainOfCustody: Generated by the emergency recovery tablet and secure workstation.
accessHistory: Stored locally on the secure workstation.
evidence.discoveryConditions: Read the export log or ask Priya about the tablet workflow.

evidence.title: Storage-embedded USB-C cable
evidence.description: A USB-C cable that appears ordinary but contains embedded storage hardware.
chainOfCustody: Present in the cable bundle near the presentation laptop.
accessHistory: Recoverable by close inspection of the cable bundle.
evidence.discoveryConditions: Closely inspect the cable bundle after identifying cable access as important.

evidence.title: Creditor message preview
evidence.description: A private message preview on Owen's phone demanding payment by morning.
chainOfCustody: Received on Owen's phone before the investor call.
accessHistory: Visible only as a notification preview or through careful questioning.
evidence.discoveryConditions: Notice the phone alert or question Owen after he reacts to it.

evidence.title: Unsent resignation draft
evidence.description: A printed draft in Lena's folder focused on disclosure ethics.
chainOfCustody: Printed by Lena before the call and kept in her folder.
accessHistory: Available in the war room folder stack.

evidence.title: Priya's audit note
evidence.description: A containment audit note showing the protocol remained inside the workstation at 5:32 p.m.
chainOfCustody: Created by Priya during the pre-call audit.
evidence.discoveryConditions: Question Priya about the audit or inspect the audit note.

scenes.openingNarration: The investor call is supposed to start in eight minutes. Instead, the secure workstation is flashing a dead-man alert: the recovery protocol may have left the room. Three people are still inside the war room, and each has a reason to want the call stopped or saved.
likelyPlayerQuestions: Can I read the alert log?
What does the recovery tablet do?
Why was Lena arguing before the call?
What was Owen doing during the projector failure?

safeAnswers.where_to_start: The alert log, export log, cable bundle, and three NPCs are all fair starting points.
safeAnswers.outside_hacker: Nothing in the room proves an outside hacker yet; the logs and cable bundle can test that theory.
safeAnswers.more_locations: The case is contained in the secure war room.

exitLeads: Compare the alert time with the export time.
Question Owen about the cable scramble.
Question Priya about the tablet and audit.
Question Lena about the investor call conflict.

scenes.entryConditions: Authored leads are exhausted or the player asks to summarize and accuse.
scenes.openingNarration: The available evidence now turns on deduction: timing, export workflow, cable access, motive, and the false lead around Lena.
likelyPlayerQuestions: Can I summarize the case board?

safeAnswers.deduction_prompt: Reconcile the alert timing, export log, cable access, physical cable, and motive.
safeAnswers.unsupported_new_lead: No new investigative source is needed; the remaining task is deduction.

hiddenFactsNotYetRevealed: Full final resolution until accusation or spoiler request.
exitLeads: Make a final accusation with culprit, motive, method, opportunity, and proof.

discoveryRules.description: Player observes the war room at the start of play.
discoveryRules.discoveryText: The failed projector and cable scramble define a short window when people moved around the secure workstation.
discoveryRules.repeatText: The same war-room leads remain available.
discoveryRules.validationNotes: Opening observation reveals only player-visible starting leads.

discoveryRules.description: Player reads the dead-man alert log.
discoveryRules.discoveryText: The alert fired at 5:48, after the export window.
discoveryRules.failureText: Without reading the alert log, the timing remains unclear.

discoveryRules.description: Player reads the diagnostic export log.
discoveryRules.discoveryText: The emergency recovery tablet initiated a diagnostic export at 5:43.
discoveryRules.failureText: Without the export log, the export method remains unclear.
discoveryRules.validationNotes: Connects method to the opportunity window.

discoveryRules.description: Player inspects the failed projector setup.
discoveryRules.discoveryText: The projector failure forced people to move near the cable bundle and workstation.
discoveryRules.repeatText: The projector failure remains the key disruption.

discoveryRules.description: Player questions Owen about the cables.
discoveryRules.discoveryText: Owen admits he handled the cables, but frames it as helping restore the call.
discoveryRules.repeatText: Owen still says he was only helping restore the call.
discoveryRules.validationNotes: Links opportunity to a specific suspect.

discoveryRules.description: Player closely inspects the cable bundle.
discoveryRules.discoveryText: One cable is heavier than expected and contains embedded storage.
discoveryRules.failureText: At a glance, the cables look ordinary. A closer inspection is needed.
discoveryRules.repeatText: The suspicious cable remains the physical exfiltration device.
discoveryRules.validationNotes: Proves method after opportunity is established.

discoveryRules.description: Player notices Owen's phone notification or his reaction to it.
discoveryRules.discoveryText: Owen's phone flashes a private payment demand before he turns it face down.
discoveryRules.failureText: Without noticing the phone, Owen's motive remains less concrete.
discoveryRules.repeatText: The phone alert remains a motive lead.
discoveryRules.validationNotes: Enables fair questioning about financial pressure.

discoveryRules.description: Player questions Owen about financial pressure.
discoveryRules.discoveryText: Owen's evasiveness around the creditor message gives him a concrete financial motive.
discoveryRules.failureText: Without a visible reason to ask about his finances, the question remains too broad.
discoveryRules.repeatText: Owen still refuses to explain the payment demand.

discoveryRules.description: Player questions Lena about opposing the call.
discoveryRules.discoveryText: Lena opposed the call because she believed the disclosure was incomplete.
discoveryRules.repeatText: Lena repeats that her dispute was about disclosure.
discoveryRules.validationNotes: Creates a fair apparent motive without proving guilt.

discoveryRules.discoveryText: The resignation draft is about ethics and disclosure, not leaking or selling files.
discoveryRules.failureText: Without reading the draft, Lena's motive remains ambiguous.
discoveryRules.repeatText: The draft still points to ethical conflict, not theft.

discoveryRules.description: Player questions Priya about her pre-call audit.
discoveryRules.discoveryText: Priya's audit places the protocol inside the workstation at 5:32.
discoveryRules.repeatText: Priya repeats that the file was still contained at 5:32.
discoveryRules.validationNotes: Narrows timeline and helps clear Priya.

discoveryRules.description: Player compares the alert and export times.
discoveryRules.discoveryText: Comparing the export and alert times shows the file was copied before the alert fired.
discoveryRules.failureText: The comparison is not meaningful until both timings are known.
discoveryRules.repeatText: The timing comparison still points to a local copy before the alert.
discoveryRules.validationNotes: Supports deduction without introducing a new clue ID.

discoveryRules.description: Player makes a final accusation against Owen.
discoveryRules.discoveryText: The accusation is supported by motive, opportunity, method, and timing.
discoveryRules.failureText: The accusation is not yet supported by enough discovered evidence.
discoveryRules.repeatText: The accusation turns on the same evidence chain.
discoveryRules.validationNotes: The GM should reveal the canonical final resolution only after the required proof threshold is met.

knownFacts: A confidential recovery protocol may have been copied from the secure workstation.
A dead-man alert fired minutes before an investor call.
The incident occurred inside the secure war room.
Three people were present: Owen, Lena, and Priya.
openQuestions: Was this an outside breach or an internal copy?
Why did the alert fire after the export window?
Who had access to the secure workstation during the projector failure?
Is Lena's opposition to the call motive or misdirection?
What did Owen handle during the cable scramble?
activeLeads: Question Owen about the projector failure.
Question Lena about the investor call argument.
Question Priya about the audit and recovery tablet.

assetManifest.title: Secure war room
assetManifest.discoveryConditions: Optional on player request; image mode is off by default.
assetManifest.textFallback: A secure conference room with a workstation, projector, whiteboard, cable bundle, and folders from the disrupted investor call.
assetManifest.imagePromptGuidance: Show only the authored room objects. No hidden culprit action, unlisted NPCs, extra rooms, extra exits, or visual clue absent from text.

assetManifest.title: Close-up of the cable bundle
assetManifest.discoveryConditions: Optional after the cable bundle has been closely inspected in text.
assetManifest.textFallback: A close-up of several USB-C cables, one slightly heavier and thicker than the others.
assetManifest.imagePromptGuidance: Support the text discovery only; do not add labels, fingerprints, names, or extra clues.

assetManifest.title: Dead-man alert log
assetManifest.discoveryConditions: Optional after the alert log is read in text.
assetManifest.textFallback: Alert: protocol package accessed at 5:48 p.m.
assetManifest.imagePromptGuidance: Show only the authored timing already revealed in text.

assetManifest.title: Risk disclosure whiteboard
assetManifest.discoveryConditions: Optional after Lena's disclosure conflict is established in text.
assetManifest.textFallback: A whiteboard with risk-disclosure notes and a circled question: 'Are we telling them enough?'
assetManifest.imagePromptGuidance: Support Lena's ethical conflict without adding hidden facts or extra documents.

knownRiskAreas: Keep image mode off by default.
Do not let background executives or hallway staff become investigative sources.
Do not introduce an outside hacker suspect as a real branch.

canonicalAssetInventory.notes: All investigative content is contained in the secure war room. Background hallway executives are atmospheric only.

runtimeBudgets.notes: Quick Mystery scope: one primary location, three major NPCs, no nested secrets.

visualDefinitions.canonicalDescription: A secure conference room with a workstation, wall projector, cable bundle, whiteboard, conference table, and folders.
visualDefinitions.fixedGeometryNotes: All gameplay remains in one glass-walled conference room; do not add extra doors, rooms, or hidden spaces.
hiddenElementRules: Do not depict storage hardware before textual discovery.
visualDefinitions.continuityAnchor: Workstation at one end of table, projector wall opposite, cable bundle near laptop.
visualDefinitions.textFallback: A secure conference room with a workstation, projector, whiteboard, cable bundle, and folders from the disrupted investor call.
visualDefinitions.validationNotes: Scene image is optional and supportive only.

visualDefinitions.canonicalDescription: A close-up of several USB-C cables, one slightly heavier and thicker than the others.
visualDefinitions.fixedGeometryNotes: Do not label the cable with culprit identity.
hiddenElementRules: Only show details already discovered in text.
visualDefinitions.textFallback: A close-up of several USB-C cables, one slightly heavier and thicker than the others.

visualDefinitions.canonicalDescription: A simple alert log showing package access at 5:48 p.m.
visualDefinitions.fixedGeometryNotes: Do not add extra log entries.
hiddenElementRules: Show only timing already revealed in text.
visualDefinitions.textFallback: Alert: protocol package accessed at 5:48 p.m.

visualDefinitions.canonicalDescription: A whiteboard with risk-disclosure notes and a circled question about whether the company is telling investors enough.
visualDefinitions.fixedGeometryNotes: Do not add new document text or accusations.
visualDefinitions.textFallback: A whiteboard with risk-disclosure notes and a circled question: 'Are we telling them enough?'
visualDefinitions.validationNotes: Red herring support, not hidden solution.

imageGalleryPolicy.notes: Assign displayed images a runtime number and reuse prior images on recall instead of regenerating.

imageReusePolicy.regenerationAllowedWhen: Only when no prior image exists or the player asks for an authored new view.
imageReusePolicy.notes: Text is canonical. If image generation cannot preserve fidelity, provide text fallback.

evidencePhotoDefinitions.canonicalDescription: A close-up of the suspicious cable after textual discovery.
evidencePhotoDefinitions.textFallback: A close-up of several USB-C cables, one slightly heavier and thicker than the others.
